title: WebAssembly Text
documentation:
## Purpose
Carries WebAssembly Text Format (WAT) source, the human-readable S-expression representation of WebAssembly bytecode. WAT is used for debugging, manual authoring of small Wasm modules, and educational purposes.

## When to use
Use `media:wat;textable;code` when a pipeline node reads or writes WAT source — for example, a `wat2wasm` compilation step, a disassembly step converting a `.wasm` binary to readable WAT, or an educational tool that generates annotated Wasm examples.

## Notes
WAT and the binary Wasm format have a 1:1 correspondence; conversion between them is lossless via tools like `wat2wasm` and `wasm2wat` from the WebAssembly Binary Toolkit (WABT). For compiled binary modules, use [WebAssembly Binary](https://capdag.com/browse/media:wasm). See also [WebAssembly Text](https://capdag.com/browse/media:wat;textable;code).
